topic: Neptune Instance Resource Allocation Overview

  entities:

    Amazon EC2|Service
    Neptune|Service
    CloudWatch|Service
    Data Flow Engine|Software
    MainRequestsPendingRequestsQueue|Software

  proposition: [Amazon EC2 instance types and sizes used in Neptune offer a defined amount of compute (vCPUs) and system memory.]

    entity-entity relationships:
    Amazon EC2|USED_IN|Neptune

    entity-attributes:
    Amazon EC2|PROVIDES|defined amount of compute (vCPUs) and system memory

  proposition: [Amazon EC2 is a compute service.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Amazon EC2|TYPE|compute service

  proposition: [Neptune is a database service.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|TYPE|database service

  proposition: [The primary storage for Neptune is external to the DB instances in a cluster.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|STORAGE_LOCATION|external to the DB instances in a cluster

  proposition: [Neptune's external primary storage allows compute and storage capacity to scale independently of each other.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|STORAGE_FEATURE|compute and storage capacity scale independently

  proposition: [In all Neptune instance families, vCPU resources are allocated to support two (2) query execution threads per vCPU.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|THREADS_PER_VCPU|2

  proposition: [The number of query execution threads supported per vCPU in Neptune is dictated by the instance size.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|THREAD_ALLOCATION|dictated by instance size

  proposition: [When determining the proper size of a Neptune DB instance, the possible concurrency of the application must be considered.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|SIZING_FACTOR|possible concurrency of the application

  proposition: [When determining the proper size of a Neptune DB instance, the average latency of queries must be considered.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|SIZING_FACTOR|average latency of queries

  proposition: [The number of vCPUs needed for a Neptune DB instance can be estimated using the formula: vCPUs = (latency x concurrency) / 2.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|VCPU_FORMULA|vCPUs = (latency x concurrency) / 2

  proposition: [In the Neptune vCPU formula, latency is measured as the average query latency in seconds.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|LATENCY_UNIT|average query latency in seconds

  proposition: [In the Neptune vCPU formula, concurrency is measured as the target number of queries per second.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|CONCURRENCY_UNIT|target number of queries per second

  proposition: [SPARQL queries can, under certain circumstances, use more than one execution thread per query.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|SPARQL_THREAD_USAGE|more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances

  proposition: [openCypher queries can, under certain circumstances, use more than one execution thread per query.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|OPENCYPHER_THREAD_USAGE|more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances

  proposition: [Gremlin read queries that use the DFE (Data Flow Engine) query engine can, under certain circumstances, use more than one execution thread per query.]

    entity-entity relationships:
    Neptune|USES|Data Flow Engine

    entity-attributes:
    Data Flow Engine|QUERY_TYPE|Gremlin read queries
    Data Flow Engine|THREAD_USAGE|more than one execution thread per query under certain circumstances

  proposition: [When initially sizing a Neptune DB cluster, the assumption should be that each query will consume a single execution thread per execution.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|SIZING_ASSUMPTION|each query consumes a single execution thread per execution

  proposition: [A Neptune DB cluster should be scaled up if back pressure into the query queue is observed.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|SCALE_UP_TRIGGER|back pressure into the query queue

  proposition: [Back pressure into the Neptune query queue can be observed using the `/gremlin/status` API.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|MONITORING_API|/gremlin/status

  proposition: [Back pressure into the Neptune query queue can be observed using the `/oc/status` API.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|MONITORING_API|/oc/status

  proposition: [Back pressure into the Neptune query queue can be observed using the `/sparql/status` API.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|MONITORING_API|/sparql/status

  proposition: [Back pressure into the Neptune query queue can be observed using the `MainRequestsPendingRequestsQueue` CloudWatch metric.]

    entity-entity relationships:
    MainRequestsPendingRequestsQueue|PART_OF|CloudWatch

    entity-attributes:
    MainRequestsPendingRequestsQueue|MONITORS|back pressure into the Neptune query queue

  proposition: [CloudWatch is a monitoring service.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    CloudWatch|TYPE|monitoring service

  proposition: [System memory on each Neptune instance is divided into two primary allocations: buffer pool cache and query execution thread memory.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|MEMORY_ALLOCATIONS|buffer pool cache and query execution thread memory

  proposition: [Approximately two thirds of the available memory in a Neptune instance is allocated for buffer pool cache.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|BUFFER_POOL_CACHE_ALLOCATION|approximately two thirds of available memory

  proposition: [Buffer pool cache is used to cache the most recently used components of the graph for faster access on queries that repeatedly access those components.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|BUFFER_POOL_CACHE_PURPOSE|cache most recently used graph components for faster repeated access

  proposition: [Neptune instances with a larger amount of system memory have larger buffer pool caches that can store more of the graph locally.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|BUFFER_POOL_CACHE_SCALING|larger system memory yields larger buffer pool cache

  proposition: [A user can tune for the appropriate amount of buffer pool cache by monitoring the buffer cache hit and miss metrics available in CloudWatch.]

    entity-entity relationships:
    Neptune|MONITORED_BY|CloudWatch

    entity-attributes:
    CloudWatch|PROVIDES_METRIC|buffer cache hit and miss metrics

  proposition: [The size of a Neptune instance should be increased if the cache hit rate drops below 99.9% for a consistent period of time.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|SCALE_UP_TRIGGER|cache hit rate drops below 99.9% for a consistent period of time

  proposition: [A cache hit rate below 99.9% for a consistent period of time suggests that the buffer pool is not big enough.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|CACHE_HIT_RATE_THRESHOLD|99.9%

  proposition: [A cache hit rate below 99.9% for a consistent period of time suggests the Neptune engine is having to fetch data from the underlying storage volume more often than is efficient.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|LOW_CACHE_HIT_IMPLICATION|frequent data fetches from underlying storage volume

  proposition: [The remaining third of system memory in a Neptune instance is distributed evenly across query execution threads.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|THREAD_MEMORY_ALLOCATION|remaining third of system memory distributed evenly across query execution threads

  proposition: [Some memory in a Neptune instance remains for the operating system.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|MEMORY_RESERVED_FOR|operating system

  proposition: [Some memory in a Neptune instance is reserved as a small dynamic pool for threads to use as needed.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|MEMORY_RESERVED_FOR|small dynamic pool for threads

  proposition: [The memory available for each Neptune query execution thread increases slightly from one instance size to the next up to an `8xl` instance type.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|THREAD_MEMORY_SCALING|increases slightly per instance size up to 8xl

  proposition: [At the `8xl` instance type, the memory allocated per thread in Neptune reaches a maximum.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|MAX_THREAD_MEMORY_INSTANCE|8xl

  proposition: [The time to add more thread memory in Neptune is when an `OutOfMemoryException` (OOM) is encountered.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|SCALE_UP_TRIGGER|OutOfMemoryException encountered

  proposition: [An `OutOfMemoryException` (OOM) in Neptune occurs when one thread needs more than the maximum memory allocated to it.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|OOM_CAUSE|one thread needs more than maximum allocated memory

  proposition: [An `OutOfMemoryException` (OOM) in Neptune is not the same as the entire instance running out of memory.]

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Neptune|OOM_CLARIFICATION|not the same as the entire instance running out of memory
